pi_items.source: OrderBookTest.java package declaration (line 1) and class name (line 13)

OrderBookTest.java contains exactly 6 @Test annotated methods: testEmptyBookHasNoBestBidOrAsk, testBestBidIsHighestPrice, testBestAskIsLowestPrice, testAtParPriceOrdersAreMatched, testCrossedPriceOrdersAreMatched, testPartialFillTracksRemainingCorrectly
setup_task.sh (line 17) records md5 checksum, export_result.sh (lines 51-52, 67-68) verifies checksum unchanged, verifier.py (lines 169-177) applies penalty if modified

Code analysis: Project is a custom Java implementation created for this educational debugging task. Contains 3 intentional bugs documented in Javadoc (Order.fillQuantity bug on line 73, OrderBook sorting bug on lines 40 and 48, MatchingEngine comparison bug on line 40). Not found in any public open-source repository. Designed as a debugging exercise.

privileged_info_summary: The trading_order_book_optimization task uses a custom Java implementation of a price-time priority matching engine with 3 intentional bugs. The project is located at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/trading-orderbook, contains a test class com.trading.OrderBookTest with 6 test methods that must all pass. The test file OrderBookTest.java must not be modified. The implementation files (Order.java, OrderBook.java, MatchingEngine.java) contain documented bugs that agents must identify and fix: (1) OrderBook uses unsorted ArrayLists for bids and asks instead of maintaining price-sorted order, (2) MatchingEngine uses strict > instead of >= for price comparison preventing at-par matches, (3) Order.fillQuantity() incorrectly mutates orderedQuantity causing getRemainingQuantity() to return incorrect values.
